prevalence.source: DataSUS and WHO diarrheal disease context
prevalence.trend: decreasing in children due to vaccination and sanitation, persistent seasonal peaks

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate in children due to dehydration risk
burden.mortalityRate: Low; higher in infants and vulnerable adults

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis (acute diarrhea/vomiting); stool testing reserved for severe, persistent, or outbreak cases

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with early oral rehydration and hygiene measures

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care evaluation; lab testing in outbreaks or severe cases
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Oral rehydration solution, zinc in children, antiemetics when appropriate; antibiotics only for specific indications
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Access to clean water and sanitation in vulnerable areas; dehydration risk in infants
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong ORS availability and rotavirus vaccination impact in infants
